rulings.Case Title: HOWARD POBLITZ et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate And Set Aside Default Judgment And Entry Of Judgment, Or Alternatively, Motion For Leave To File Complaint In Intervention
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, December 20, 2018 in Department 503, Line 1. Great American Insurance Company's Motion to Vacate and Set Aside Default Judgment and Entry of Judgment is off calendar per the stipulation by parties. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)

rulings.Case Title: SHARON JENNINGS ET AL VS. HONEYWELL INTERNATIONAL, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint Against Caterpillar, Inc.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, December 20, 2018 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant Quinn Company's Motion for Leave to File Cross-Complaint Against Caterpillar, Inc. is denied. "Except as between plaintiffs and defendants, there is no compulsory cross-complaint in California procedure . . .. Rather a cross-complaint is permissive as between co-parties . . .." (Banerian v. O'Malley (1974) 42 Cal.App.3d 604, 612.) If a proposed cross-complaint is permissive, leave of the court may be granted "in the interest of justice" at any time during the course of the action. (CCP § 428.50(c).) The Court finds that the interest of justice does not support leave for Quinn Company to file its cross-complaint. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)

rulings.Case Title: SHARON JENNINGS ET AL VS. HONEYWELL INTERNATIONAL, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, December 20, 2018 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant Quinn Company's Motion for Summary Judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that Plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to Defendant. The deposition testimony of Oscar Esquer preclude Defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Company (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826 and Scheiding v. Dinwiddie Construction Company (1999) 69 Cal.App.4th 76. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)
